chunks.text: Windows | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Windows The EECS department maintains a large and comprehensive Windows environment, which is tightly integrated with our Unix environment. The EECS Active Directo

with our Unix environment. The EECS Active Directory replaced obsolete NT4 domains, substantially streamlined the Windows environment, and offered more advanced features and services. Some highlights of interest to the general user include: Easy inst

of interest to the general user include: Easy installation of software without needing to log in as administrator. OS installation over the network. Integrated sign-on to Windows machines throughout the department. We maintain quite a bit of document

he department. We maintain quite a bit of documentation regarding Windows including installation , software usage , and even who to call in case you get stuck. See also: How do I set up printing? There’s no print queue on \\print for the printer I wa

e’s no print queue on \\print for the printer I want to use. What do I do? I can’t find [your software package here] on \\winsww. Can you put it on? How do I set my screen resolution? Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, an
IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
